findings.description: The photography style requirement (warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery depicting everyday activities) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set. This is a distinct imagery directive that should be extracted as its own rule, not omitted.

payload.description: Photography must depict warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery showing everyday activities (e.g., walking the dog, beach, BBQ), conveying return to normal life.

findings.description: The requirement that images be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles or circles) is a distinct hard constraint referencing §2.5 that is missing from the candidate rules entirely.

payload.description: Images must be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crops per §2.5); plain rectangles or circles are prohibited.

findings.description: The alt text rule is flagged as a 'brand accessibility rule' but no governance facet (e.g., referenced standard like WCAG, owning team, or enforcement mechanism) is captured, leaving the compliance basis unclear.

payload.governance_note: Sourced from brand accessibility rule; no specific standard (e.g., WCAG) or owning body cited in source — should be flagged for governance clarification.

findings.description: Hardness (soft_guidance) and polarity (may) correctly reflect the permissive source language 'Waves and gradients may overlay photography' — no error, included for completeness of review.

The candidate group only covers 2 of 4 content-bearing units under 'Image Treatment', indicating under-extraction; the group should be expanded to include all four distinct rules (photography style, accent-shape housing, wave/gradient overlay, alt text) for complete coverage of the heading's scope.
